title: Coppensia pirarensis
taxonomy: Coppensia pirarensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in the countries of Brazil and Peru. The classification of Coppensia pirarensis places it within the kingdom Plantae, and further categorizes it as a member of the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's specific epithet, pirarensis, refers to its origin in the Piraremirim River in northeastern Brazil. The plant's classification is used in botanical research and conservation efforts to understand its unique characteristics and ecological role.

Coppensia pirarensis has been studied for its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
